evidence: Big Oh notation \(O(g(n))\) intuitively represents an **upper bound on the growth rate** of a function.
If \(f(n) \in O(g(n))\), then for sufficiently large \(n\), \(f(n)\) does not grow faster than a constant multiple of \(g(n)\).
It is used to describe the **asymptotic worst-case growth** of an algorithm or function
\(f(n)\) stays at or below \(c \cdot g(n)\) for some constant \(c > 0\)
